summary: PR 2605 links the small remaining `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` object by shrinking its split to 0x8010A930-0x8010AA64, moving the former tail range 0x8010AA64-0x8010D740 into a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`/`.h` nonmatching object, and updating config, split metadata, symbol metadata, includes, motion-state references, and item references accordingly.

key_files.role: Adjusted `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` text/sdata2 range and introduced `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` split range.
Changed several `.sdata2` float symbols at 0x804D9558-0x804D9564 from global `ftKb_Init_*` names to local `@` labels.
Changed `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` from NonMatching to Matching and added `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` as NonMatching.
Updated Kirby init include from `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` to `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h`.
Reduced source file to the linked capture-yoshi portion ending at `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AA2C`; removed the moved Yoshi egg / Marth-Roy copied special-N code.
Removed old header declarations for the larger combined file.
New source file containing the moved former tail of `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c`.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c deletion implies moved content; changed_files.jsonl shows 1459 added lines

key_files.role: New header corresponding to the moved Yoshi egg code.
One-line reference update associated with the new split/header arrangement.
Added one line, likely to reference the new Yoshi egg header/source surface; exact line not visible in excerpt.
One-line reference update associated with moved Kirby/Yoshi egg declarations.
One-line reference update associated with moved Kirby/Yoshi egg declarations.

what_changed: The PR metadata reports 12 changed files, +1576/-1544 lines, with no PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews.
`configure.py` changed `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` and added `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.

`ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` was reduced from the larger combined file to the first functions through `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AA2C`; the deleted excerpt includes `fn_8010AA64`, `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AC78`, `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*`, and many later Kirby special-N functions.
The moved code was represented by a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` added with 1459 lines and a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` added with 100 lines.

smart_moves.text: The PR isolates a small matching front range rather than keeping the entire old combined source as one nonmatching object.
The old tail code is not discarded; it is preserved under a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` nonmatching object with its own header and split range.
Metadata was updated together with the source movement: splits, symbols, object matching status, and include users changed in the same PR.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a front portion of a large nonmatching TU can be linked, a narrow split can allow that portion to become `Matching` while the remaining range moves to a new `NonMatching` object.
A source-file split for matching work should be accompanied by config/split updates and include/reference updates, not just by moving C code.

naming_conventions.text: The new file/header name `ftkirbyyoshiegg` reflects the visible moved common-state functions named `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*`.
The PR preserves address-style function names such as `fn_8010A930` and `ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010AA2C` where no semantic rename evidence is provided.
Float symbols in the affected `.sdata2` range were changed from global `ftKb_Init_804D9558`-style names to local `@223`/`@248`/`@224`/`@225` labels.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use split-boundary refinement to link a subset of a previously nonmatching translation unit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR does not include objdiff or build logs in the loaded evidence; the tactic is inferred from the matching-status/config change and PR title.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Move the remaining nonmatching code into a new object so the matched object can have a smaller `.text`/`.sdata2` ownership range.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is supported by the old range ending at 0x8010D740, the new capture range ending at 0x8010AA64, and the new Yoshi egg range beginning at 0x8010AA64.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust symbol metadata for section ownership when splitting the TU, including localizing affected `.sdata2` float labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The loaded evidence shows the symbol changes but not the underlying symbol-discovery rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Link a subset of a nonmatching TU by splitting out the remaining range
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2605 shows a pattern where `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` became `Matching` only after its split range was narrowed to 0x8010A930-0x8010AA64 and the old tail range was moved to a new `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` `NonMatching` object.

source_update_candidates.title: PR 2605 split `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` and introduced `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c`
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2605, `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` was narrowed to `.text` 0x8010A930-0x8010AA64 and `.sdata2` 0x804D9560-0x804D9568; `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` was added for `.text` 0x8010AA64-0x8010D740 and `.sdata2` 0x804D9568-0x804D95B0.
